respectful_language.core_principle: Image-bearers must not be degraded. Love confronts sin and problems without contempt, mockery, scorning, or speech that strips dignity.

love_reframe.identity: I want my words to feel safe, honest, and honoring.
love_reframe.motive_shift: Not: 'I should stop being harsh.' → But: 'I love you, so I will guard your dignity even when I need to address harm.'
replacement_focus: I will describe actions and impact instead of attacking character.
I will slow down before speaking when I feel heat rising.
I will choose words that are truthful and healing, not humiliating.
If I speak with contempt, I will repair directly and specifically.

self_application_prayer: Lord, keep contempt and corruption far from my mouth.
Teach me to speak hard truths with gentleness and honor.

respectful_listening.sin_label: Prideful interruption / failure to honor another's voice
respectful_listening.core_principle: Listening is an act of humility and honor. Love does not overpower, dominate the floor, or rush past another person's pace.

love_reframe.identity: I want you to experience being heard, not competed with.
love_reframe.motive_shift: Not: 'I shouldn't interrupt.' → But: 'I love you, so I will make room for your words, timing, and meaning.'
replacement_focus: I will let you finish before I respond.
I will summarize what I heard before defending or redirecting.
I will ask clarifying questions instead of assuming I already know.
I will treat listening as service, not passivity.

self_application_prayer: Lord, make me humble, patient, and teachable.
Help me listen to understand rather than to win.

truthful_communication.core_principle: Love honors truth by being specific, fair, and reality-based. Exaggeration, mind-reading, selective framing, and careless accusations distort shared reality.

love_reframe.identity: I want my words to protect reality, not bend it.
love_reframe.motive_shift: Not: 'I shouldn't exaggerate.' → But: 'I love you, so I will be fair, concrete, and careful with what I say happened.'
replacement_focus: I will trade labels for observations.
I will name uncertainty when I do not know motives.
I will avoid all-or-nothing language when it is not true.
I will correct the record quickly when I overstate or misstate.

self_application_prayer: Lord, make me careful with truth and quick to correct error.
Guard me from careless speech that harms shared reality.

no_lying_or_omission.core_principle: God is truth. Love does not hide, mislead, or selectively reveal reality to preserve image, control outcomes, or avoid consequences.

love_reframe.identity: I want my honesty to be a shelter, not a fog.
love_reframe.motive_shift: Not: 'I shouldn't lie.' → But: 'I love you, so I will protect trust with whole truth, not curated truth.'
replacement_focus: I will tell the whole truth, not only the least damaging part.
I will name uncertainty instead of shaping the story.
I will confess sooner rather than waiting for discovery.
I will remember that truth is part of safety.

self_application_prayer: Lord, make me a person of truth in private and public.
Give me courage to tell the truth without self-protection.

promise_integrity.core_principle: Love is trustworthy and carries weight. Repeated broken commitments erode safety, peace, and confidence in relationship.

love_reframe.identity: I want my words and follow-through to match.
love_reframe.motive_shift: Not: 'I should remember better.' → But: 'I love you, so I will treat my commitments as part of your safety and peace.'
replacement_focus: I will promise less and follow through more.
I will communicate early when I cannot fulfill what I said.
I will use reminders and systems because love plans for faithfulness.
I will repair specifically when I break trust.

self_application_prayer: Lord, make me dependable and faithful in small and great things.
Teach me integrity that does not collapse under pressure.

pauses_equal_abandonment.core_principle: Love may need pauses, but it does not leave the other person in fear, silence, or uncertainty without orientation.

love_reframe.identity: I want my pauses to feel safe, not punishing or abandoning.
love_reframe.motive_shift: Not: 'I shouldn't go silent.' → But: 'I love you, so I will regulate without leaving you alone in uncertainty.'
replacement_focus: I can pause, but I will anchor you with a clear return time.
I will signal care while I regulate.
I will close loops so your nervous system is not forced to guess.
I will distinguish needed space from withholding.

self_application_prayer: Lord, teach me steady presence under stress.
Help me pause without withdrawing love.

transition_protocol.core_principle: Peace is protected through clarity, order, and orientation. Love does not disappear without helping the other person understand what is happening next.

love_reframe.identity: I want my exits and returns to communicate safety.
love_reframe.motive_shift: Not: 'I should explain myself.' → But: 'I love you, so I will give orientation, timing, and peace.'
replacement_focus: I will name what is happening and what happens next.
I will give a concrete return point when I need to step away.
I will not vanish in the middle of unresolved distress.
I will follow through because order is part of love.

self_application_prayer: Lord, make me orderly, gentle, and clear.
Help me bring peace instead of confusion in my movements.

close_the_loop.core_principle: Love does not treat unfinished repair as neutral. Open loops keep harm alive; godly love moves toward completion, clarification, and peace.

love_reframe.identity: I want repair to become complete, not half-open and drifting.
love_reframe.motive_shift: Not: 'I should probably revisit this.' → But: 'I love you, so I will not leave you carrying unresolved harm alone.'
replacement_focus: I will revisit unresolved issues at the agreed time.
I will clarify what has and has not been repaired.
I will not call something finished just because I feel tired.
I will value completion as part of peace.

self_application_prayer: Lord, make me willing to finish what repair requires.
Teach me perseverance in reconciliation.

no_blameshifting.core_principle: Love owns its own sin and impact. Blame-shifting hides from repentance by relocating responsibility onto the harmed person, circumstances, or stress.

love_reframe.identity: I want my ownership to be clear and honest.
love_reframe.motive_shift: Not: 'I shouldn't blame-shift.' → But: 'I love you, so I will not protect myself by placing my responsibility onto you.'
replacement_focus: I will name what I did before I explain context.
I will separate causes from excuses.
I will avoid 'but you' after confession.
I will let ownership be specific, concrete, and complete.

self_application_prayer: Lord, make me honest about my own sin and impact.
Remove self-justification and teach me clean confession.

no_defensiveness.core_principle: Defensiveness blocks listening, obscures empathy, and protects ego at the expense of repair. Love makes room for the other's pain before self-explanation.

love_reframe.identity: I want your pain to meet listening, not argument.
love_reframe.motive_shift: Not: 'I shouldn't defend.' → But: 'I love you, so I will make room for your experience before I move toward explanation.'
replacement_focus: I will reflect impact first.
I will ask what is most painful right now.
I will save explanation for the moment when it truly serves clarity.
I will remember that understanding is not the same as agreeing with every interpretation.

self_application_prayer: Lord, soften my heart and quiet my self-defense.
Teach me to listen like You listen.

emotional_presence.sin_label: Neglect / withholding care / failure to draw near
emotional_presence.core_principle: Love draws near in difficulty. Presence, attunement, and burden-bearing are forms of service, not optional extras.

love_reframe.identity: I want my attention to communicate: you matter.
love_reframe.motive_shift: Not: 'I should be more present.' → But: 'I love you, so I will draw near with attention, care, and steadiness.'
replacement_focus: I will notice and respond when distress is visible.
I will ask follow-up questions instead of staying passive.
I will let presence be practical, not merely verbal.
I will treat attention as part of covenant care.

self_application_prayer: Lord, make me attentive and near-hearted.
Teach me to show love through steady presence.

no_manipulation.core_principle: Love does not coerce, corner, pressure, or use fear, guilt, silence, spirituality, or relational leverage to control another person's response.

love_reframe.identity: I want your freedom and consent to be real, not extracted.
love_reframe.motive_shift: Not: 'I shouldn't control.' → But: 'I love you, so I will protect your agency, clarity, and safety instead of forcing outcomes.'
replacement_focus: I will ask instead of cornering.
I will remove leverage instead of increasing pressure.
I will accept no without punishment.
I will pursue influence through truth and trust, not force.

self_application_prayer: Lord, remove control and fear from my heart.
Teach me love that honors the other person's agency.

respect_boundaries.core_principle: Love honors limits, pace, consent, and personhood. Boundaries are not the enemy of love; they are one way love protects safety and stewardship.

love_reframe.identity: I want your 'no,' your pace, and your limits to be safe with me.
love_reframe.motive_shift: Not: 'I shouldn't push.' → But: 'I love you, so I will protect your safety by honoring your boundaries immediately and consistently.'
replacement_focus: I will stop when you say no.
I will not argue boundaries in the moment.
I will not spiritualize, guilt, or pressure you past your limits.
I will prove safety through consistent follow-through.

self_application_prayer: Lord, teach me to honor limits as love.
Help me prefer faithfulness over urgency.

spiritual_manipulation.sin_label: Using Scripture or theology as control
spiritual_manipulation.core_principle: God's Word is for truth, healing, repentance, and formation—not for domination, deflection, silencing, or burdening another person while avoiding one's own accountability.

love_reframe.identity: I want God's truth to be a light, not a weapon, in my hands.
love_reframe.motive_shift: Not: 'I shouldn't use Bible verses like that.' → But: 'I love you, so I will use truth to serve repentance and healing, not to control, shame, or escape accountability.'
replacement_focus: I will apply Scripture to myself before I use it to confront you.
I will not quote truth to avoid owning my behavior.
I will refuse theological superiority in conflict.
I will let gentleness and integrity govern how I use spiritual language.

self_application_prayer: Lord, keep me from using Your Word to protect my ego.
Make me gentle, truthful, and accountable in how I speak about You.
